citations.quote: oversight of crypto will continue to be limited to financial promotions and anti-money laundering controls
The full scope of regulated activities under the regime will expand from 25 October 2027
the application period will open on 30 September 2026

summary: On 30 June 2026 the FCA published five final policy statements (PS26/9-PS26/13) completing its rulebook for the UK's new cryptoasset regime, created by the Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (Cryptoassets) Regulations 2026 (2026 No. 102). Together they finalise conduct, market-abuse, prudential and Handbook-application rules for firms operating a qualifying cryptoasset trading platform (QCATP), dealing in or safeguarding qualifying cryptoassets, staking, and issuing a qualifying stablecoin backed by segregated reserve assets. The rules are finalised, but the regime itself is not yet in force: the FCA states the new mandatory regime commences on 25 October 2027, and until then its oversight of crypto remains limited to financial promotions standards and anti-money-laundering supervision under the Money Laundering Regulations 2017. The authorisation gateway is expected to open on 30 September 2026.
title: FCA sets landmark crypto rules to cement the UK's place as a global hub
why_it_matters: This is the FCA's completed rulebook for how UK crypto exchanges, custodians, dealers and stablecoin issuers will eventually need to be licensed -- but none of it is enforceable yet. Until the regime commences on 25 October 2027, the FCA's only live powers over UK crypto firms are financial promotions standards and anti-money-laundering registration, not this new licensing framework.
